title: Junta Airstrikes Hit Five Villages in Nyaunglebin District, Bago Region
detail: Between March 19 and 22, junta forces repeatedly bombed Pho Ta, Shwe Hte Su, Shwe Khar Moe, Dala Seik, and Lein Pin We villages in Ledo (Kyaukkyi) Township, Nyaunglebin District, using drones and Y-12 aircraft. In just two days (March 21–22), Kler Lwee Htoo District recorded 23 Y-12 strikes, 30 rounds of 120mm artillery, and 4 drone attacks, forcing mass civilian displacement. On March 21 alone, a Y-12 strike on Shwe Khe Su village in Saw Htee Township injured one civilian, a 47-year-old man identified as Saw Thay Lar Hae. The Karen National Union (KNU) documented 16 civilian homes, 2 cattle sheds, and 1 oil mill destroyed across the strikes.

title: International Experts Call for Global Rejection of Junta's 'Puppet Parliament'
detail: Justice for Myanmar (JFM) and a coalition of international human rights experts issued a formal statement on March 21, 2026, urging all governments to unequivocally reject the junta's newly convened parliament as illegitimate. The Special Advisory Council for Myanmar (SAC-M) declared that 'the junta's expectation that its sham elections could lead, by stealth, to the formation of a legitimate parliament readily accepted by the international community is delusional.' SAC-M called on states, the UN, and international bodies to increase financial, material, and capacity-building support for the NUG, ethnic organizations, and civil society as the rightful representatives of Myanmar's people.
